Zoho Creator

Right-to-Left
Design Standards
&Product Audit

A comprehensive review of RTL design principles with visual references, mapped against our current Arabic implementation to identify gaps and priorities.

RTL Review RTL Standards Priorities Forms Reports Calendar Dialogs Integrations Map Checklist
Part One

RTL Design Standards

The foundational principles every RTL interface must follow, with visual references showing correct implementation.

LTR ✗
RTL ✓

01 Layout Mirroring

The entire page layout mirrors horizontally. Sidebars move from left to right. Content flows right-to-left. Navigation, toolbars, and action bars reverse their position.

Sidebar RIGHT · Toolbar RIGHT-aligned · Scrollbar LEFT · Close button TOP-LEFT
LTR ✗
Full Name
John Smith
Email
[email  protected]
RTL ✓
الاسم الكامل
محمد أحمد
البريد الإلكتروني
[email  protected]

02 Text & Input Direction

Labels and content right-align. Input fields flow right-to-left for Arabic text. Special content like emails, URLs, and code must retain LTR direction using dir="ltr" wrapping.

Labels RIGHT-aligned · Arabic input RTL · Emails/URLs wrapped in dir="ltr" · Placeholders translated
LTR ✗
Option A
Choice 1
Choice 2
RTL ✓
الخيار أ
الاختيار ١
الاختيار ٢

03 Form Controls Position

Checkboxes, radio buttons, and toggle switches appear on the start side of their labels — RIGHT in RTL. Dropdown carets move to the end side (LEFT). All interactive affordances mirror.

Checkbox RIGHT of label · Radio RIGHT of label · Dropdown caret on LEFT · Toggle RIGHT of label
LTR ✗
🇮🇳 +91 ˅
81234 56789
Flag on LEFT ✗
RTL ✓
🇮🇳 +91 ˅
81234 56789
Flag on RIGHT ✓

04 Composite Fields

Phone fields: country flag+code anchors to the RIGHT (start). Name fields reverse sub-field order (Suffix → Last → First → Prefix). Address sub-labels must be translated. Number inputs retain LTR digit direction.

Phone flag RIGHT · Name fields reversed · Address labels Arabic · Numbers dir="ltr"
← →
Back / Forward
Swap in RTL
▶ ⏸
Play / Pause
Keep as-is
✏️ 🔍
Edit / Search
Keep as-is
Breadcrumbs LTR: Home › Settings › Profile → الملف الشخصي ‹ الإعدادات ‹ الرئيسية

05 Icons & Directional Elements

Navigation arrows (back/forward, breadcrumb separators) must mirror. Media controls (play, pause, volume) do NOT mirror — they represent physical direction. Symmetric icons (search, edit, delete) stay unchanged.

← → swap · ‹ › breadcrumbs flip · ▶ stays · 🔍 stays · Progress bars flip
Type English LTR ✗ Arabic RTL ✓
Date Jan 23, 2025 ٢٣ يناير ٢٠٢٥
Currency SAR 1,234.56 ١٬٢٣٤٫٥٦ ر.س
Phone +91 81234 56789 +91 81234 56789
Time 4:12 PM ٤:١٢ م

06 Numbers, Dates & Currency

Dates use Arabic month names and optionally Eastern Arabic numerals (٠١٢٣٤٥٦٧٨٩). Currency symbols follow the number. Phone numbers and coordinates must be wrapped in LTR. AM/PM becomes ص/م.

٢٣ يناير ٢٠٢٥ · ١٬٢٣٤٫٥٦ ر.س · ٤:١٢ م · Phone dir="ltr"
LTR ✗
Sun
Mon
Tue
Wed
Thu
Fri
Sat
1
2
3
4
5
6
7
‹ Prev Feb 2026 Next ›
RTL ✓
سبت
جمع
خمي
أرب
ثلا
إثن
أحد
٧
٦
٥
٤
٣
٢
١
التالي ‹ فبراير ٢٠٢٦ › السابق

07 Calendar & Scheduling

The calendar grid mirrors completely — Saturday on the far LEFT, days flow right-to-left. Day names in Arabic. Navigation arrows swap: ‹ means "next" in RTL. Month/year names use Arabic. Day numbers optionally use Eastern Arabic numerals.

Grid mirrored · Sat LEFT · Day names Arabic · ‹ = Next · › = Previous
المبيعات
٧٥٪
الدعم
٤٥٪
التسويق
٦٠٪

08 Charts & Data Visualization

Bar charts fill from RIGHT to LEFT. Y-axis labels move to the RIGHT. X-axis labels reverse order. Progress bars fill RTL. Data labels use Eastern Arabic numerals. Column headers and legends must be translated.

Bars fill R→L · Y-axis RIGHT · Labels Arabic · ٧٥٪ not 75% · Legends translated
✗ COMMON FAILURE
Embedded widgets (file pickers, map controls, rich text editors) render 100% LTR English, ignoring the parent app's dir="rtl" and locale settings.
✓ REQUIREMENT
Third-party components must inherit dir="rtl" and lang="ar" . If the vendor doesn't support RTL, a custom wrapper must override layout, translate labels, and mirror controls.

09 Third-Party Components

Embedded widgets (file pickers, cloud connectors, rich text editors, map controls) must respect the app's RTL direction. All system labels, buttons, and placeholders within these widgets must be translated.

Cloud pickers · File browsers · Map layer selectors · Rich text toolbars · WYSIWYG editors
Aa
English 14px
→
أب
Arabic 14px ✗
→
أب
Arabic 16px ✓
Arabic text needs +2pt size increase over Latin · Never apply letter-spacing to Arabic (breaks ligatures)

10 Typography & Font Handling

Arabic script requires a dedicated font (Noto Sans Arabic, Noto Kufi, etc.). Font size should increase by ~2pt over Latin equivalents for equal readability. Never apply letter-spacing to Arabic — it disconnects cursive ligatures.

Dedicated Arabic font · +2pt size · No letter-spacing · line-height: 1.8+ · text-align: right
✗ AVOID — Hardcoded Direction
margin-left : 16px;
padding-right : 12px;
text-align: left ;
✓ USE — Logical Properties
margin-inline-start : 16px;
padding-inline-end : 12px;
text-align: start ;

11 Leading & Trailing (Dev)

Never use hardcoded left /right in CSS or layout code. Use logical properties: start , end , inline-start , inline-end , leading , trailing . This ensures automatic mirroring when the direction switches to RTL.

margin-inline-start · padding-inline-end · inset-inline · text-align: start · border-start-start-radius
RTL UI — Arabic Paragraph ✓
هذا نص عربي طويل يتكون من عدة أسطر ومحاذاته صحيحة إلى اليمين في واجهة من اليمين إلى اليسار.
RTL UI — English Paragraph ✓
This English paragraph stays left-aligned inside an RTL UI because readability depends on the starting edge.
RTL UI — English Right-Aligned ✗
This English text is right-aligned in RTL and becomes very hard to read naturally.

12 Paragraph Alignment Rule

A paragraph (3+ lines) must align to its own language , not the UI direction. Arabic paragraphs right-align in RTL. English paragraphs must stay left-aligned even inside an RTL interface — forcing English text to the right breaks readability.

Arabic paragraph → right-aligned · English paragraph in RTL → left-aligned · Per-language alignment · Not per-UI direction
WRONG ✗
• الخيار الأول
• Option Two
• الخيار الثالث
Mixed alignment ✗
CORRECT ✓
• الخيار الأول
• الخيار الثاني
• الخيار الثالث
Consistent alignment ✓

13 List Alignment Consistency

All items in a list must follow the same alignment. In RTL, every item should be right-aligned. Mixing left and right alignment within a single list breaks visual scanning rhythm and looks inconsistent to users.

All items same alignment · No mixed directions within one list · Improves scanning · Consistent visual rhythm
REVERSE DIRECTION, NOT DIGITS
LTR — Stepper
1 → 2 → 3
RTL — Stepper
1 ← 2 ← 3
LTR — Rating Stars
★★★★★
1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5
RTL — Rating Stars
★★★★★
5 ← 4 ← 3 ← 2 ← 1
LTR — Pagination
1 2 3 ›
RTL — Pagination
1 2 3 ‹

14 Progression Direction

UI elements showing order or progress — steppers, rating stars, sliders, progress bars, and pagination — must reverse their direction in RTL, but the digit values themselves must never be reversed. Step 1 is still "1", it just starts on the right.

Steppers flow R→L · Stars fill from right · Pagination starts right · Slider fills R→L · Digits unchanged
🧭
Physical direction
Do NOT flip
↩️
Reading direction
DO flip in RTL
Keep unchanged: "Turn Right" ➜, "Swipe Left" ⬅, physical map directions, compass indicators, real-world movement instructions.
Mirror in RTL: "Go Back" ←, "Next" →, "Previous" ←, breadcrumb arrows, navigation flow.

15 Real-World vs Reading Direction

Buttons or icons representing physical/real-world direction ("Turn Right", "Move Left") must remain physically accurate and never flip. RTL mirroring only applies to reading and navigation direction — not to spatial or geographic meaning.

"Turn Right" stays → · "Go Back" flips · Map directions unchanged · Navigation arrows mirror
🖼️
Original ✓
≠
🖼️
Flipped ✗
Never flip: Photos, maps, real-world objects, copyrighted images, screenshots.
Do reverse: The position of images in ordered sequences (chronological, ranked, favorites) — reverse the order, not the content.

16 Images — Position, Not Content

Images must never be flipped horizontally in RTL. Flipping photos, maps, or copyrighted images changes meaning and can cause confusion or legal issues. When images appear in a meaningful sequence, reverse their position order only.

Photos never flip · Maps never flip · Chronological order reverses · Ranked order reverses · Content stays original
🔔3
Badge icon
Reposition badge
📁🔗
Overlay icon
Evaluate individually
🔇
Slash icon
Check slash direction
Complex icons with badges , slashes , overlays , or tool indicators need case-by-case evaluation — don't blindly mirror them.

17 Complex Multi-Element Icons

Icons containing badges, slashes, overlays, or sub-elements must not be blindly mirrored. Evaluate each part: should the base image flip? Should the badge reposition? Does visual balance still hold? Complex icons require individual RTL assessment.

Badge repositions · Slash direction evaluated · Overlay adjusts · Base may or may not flip · Never auto-mirror blindly
🛡️
Brand Identity
Never change
🔖
Logo Orientation
Never flip
⚖️
Legal Marks
Never modify
🔢
Number Meaning
Never reverse

18 What Must Never Change

RTL must preserve meaning. Brand identity, logo orientation, legal marks, universal symbols (✓, ✗, ⚠), and the inherent meaning of numbers must remain completely untouched regardless of UI direction.

Logos never flip · ™ © ® untouched · Checkmarks universal · Number sequences preserved · Brand colors unchanged
Mandatory

RTL Testing Checklist

Every screen must pass both design and development review before release. This checklist covers the critical verification points.

DESIGN Design Review Checklist

Layout mirrors correctly (sidebar, nav, actions)
All text aligns properly per language
Paragraph alignment follows content language
Lists are consistently aligned
Navigation direction is correct
Directional icons flipped appropriately
Logos and brand marks remain unchanged
Visual balance maintained (Arabic font sizing)
Images not flipped — only repositioned

DEV Development Review Checklist

No hardcoded left/right CSS values
Leading/trailing logical properties used
Directional controls reverse correctly
Numbers are never reversed
All screens tested in RTL mode
Emails, URLs, phone numbers wrapped in dir="ltr"
Third-party components inherit dir="rtl"
Steppers, pagination, sliders reverse direction
Arabic font loaded and sized correctly (+2pt)
Part Two

Product Insights — Overview

Summary of all issues found in our Arabic RTL implementation, organized by priority.

21
Screens Audited
20
Critical Issues
37
High Issues
8
Medium / Low

Top Priority Actions

1

Mirror All Checkbox & Radio Button Controls

Move from LEFT to RIGHT of labels. This is the single most repeated issue — appears in Forms, Search panels, Export dialogs, Filter panels, and PDF Export settings across 8+ screens.

Systemic · 8+ screens
2

Translate Address & Name Sub-Labels

"Address Line 1", "City / District", "Prefix", "First Name" appear English in every context — form builder, search filters, and report views. One translation pass fixes this across the platform.

Systemic · Forms + Search + Reports
3

Fix Cloud Picker & File Browser RTL

Cloud Picker is 100% English with zero Arabic. The embedded file browser ignores dir="rtl" entirely. Requires third-party component update or custom wrapper.

Critical · Completely broken
4

Mirror Calendar Grid Direction

Calendar days flow LTR (Sunday LEFT → Saturday RIGHT). Must reverse. Month picker grid also flows LTR. Navigation arrows need position/meaning swap.

High · Calendar + Month Picker + Timeline
5

Translate Report Headers, Kanban Labels & Empty States

"rose subform", "Choice 1/2/3", "Deleted & Others", "- Empty -", "No Records in..." — all report-facing labels stay English.

Critical · All report types
6

Fix Phone Field Flag Position

Country flag+code on LEFT in every phone instance. Must move to RIGHT (start position in RTL). Confirmed systemic across 3+ instances.

High · All phone fields
Part Three

Product Evidence — By Area

Each functional area shows the applicable RTL standard, followed by annotated screenshots from our live product identifying specific violations.

📝

Form Components

Standards 02, 03, 04

All form labels, sub-labels, and placeholders must be translated. Checkboxes and radio buttons appear RIGHT of labels. Composite fields (Name, Address, Phone) reverse sub-field order. Dropdown carets mirror to LEFT (end position).

SCREEN 01 Form Builder — Name, Phone, Address Fields
CRITICAL

Name sub-labels ALL English

"Prefix", "First Name", "Last Name", "Suffix" not translated. Sub-field order should reverse for RTL reading.

CRITICAL

Phone flag+code on LEFT side

🇮🇳 +91 selector on LEFT. Must anchor to RIGHT in RTL.

HIGH

Address sub-labels ALL English

Six sub-labels untranslated: Address Line 1/2, City/District, State/Province, Country, Postal Code.

HIGH

Dropdown carets on wrong side

All dropdowns show chevron (˅) on LEFT without mirroring the full component.

MEDIUM

"Add your Note here..." English placeholder

Rich text placeholder not translated.

SCREEN 02 Address Continuation & Subform Table
CRITICAL

Country dropdown: English names, LTR truncated

"...an Samoa" — English name cut from LEFT. Must show Arabic country names with RTL truncation.

HIGH

GPS coordinates BiDi scrambled

"-14.27097-" — negative signs duplicated by BiDi algorithm. Need dir="ltr" wrapping.

HIGH

Machine translation inconsistencies

"مقاطعه/المدينه" vs "الولاية/المقاطعة" — inconsistent Arabic grammar.

SCREEN 03 Time Picker, Radio Buttons & Upload
CRITICAL

"Time" label not translated

Displays English "Time" instead of "الوقت".

CRITICAL

Radio buttons — circles on LEFT

الاختيار 1/2/3 with radio circles on LEFT. Must be RIGHT in RTL.

HIGH

Time picker fully LTR

AM/PM English, Western numerals, no right-alignment.

HIGH

Upload icon on wrong side

Upload ↑ icon LEFT of "تحديد صورة". Should be RIGHT.

SCREEN 04 Edit Mode — All Field Types Overview
CRITICAL

Rich text toolbar LTR

B/I/U buttons and "DejaVuSans" font in LTR layout. No Arabic font option.

CRITICAL

Bottom field type bar mixed language

Field types flow LTR instead of RTL.

HIGH

Checkbox on LEFT of labels

"صندوق الاقرار" checkbox LEFT of label. Confirmed systemic.

HIGH

Phone flag on LEFT (systemic)

Second phone instance confirms component-level bug.

📊

Reports & Data Views

Standards 06, 08

Report headers, column names, dates, and empty states must be translated. Data tables mirror column order. Dates use Arabic month names. Kanban column labels and card layouts must follow RTL.

SCREEN 05 List Report — Data Table
CRITICAL

Column header "rose subform" English

Report column headers not translated.

CRITICAL

Date: "September-23" English month + LTR arrows

Must use Arabic month names with mirrored navigation.

HIGH

Tooltip anchored to wrong side

"تكوين ترتيب مخصص" tooltip pops from LEFT-side icons.

HIGH

Latin text lacks dir="ltr" wrapper

Lorem Ipsum right-aligned in RTL container needs explicit LTR direction.

SCREEN 06 Kanban Report
CRITICAL

ALL column headers English

"Choice 1", "Choice 2", "Choice 3", "Deleted & Others" never translate.

HIGH

Empty state messages English

"No Records in Deleted & Others" — system labels untranslated.

HIGH

Card text LTR-truncated

Truncation follows LTR pattern in RTL cards.

SCREEN 07 Kanban with Real Data
CRITICAL

"- Empty -" column label English

System labels and count badges use Western numerals.

HIGH

Card thumbnails on LEFT side

Images should be RIGHT (start of reading flow) in RTL cards.

HIGH

Empty state: "No Records in Choice 1" English

Confirms systemic untranslated empty states.

📅

Calendar & Timeline

Standard 07

Calendar grid mirrors: Saturday on far LEFT, days flow RTL. Day names in Arabic. Navigation arrows swap meaning. Month picker and timeline grids also mirror.

SCREEN 08 Calendar Report — Monthly View
CRITICAL

Calendar days flow LTR

Sunday (الأحد) on far LEFT, Saturday (السبت) far RIGHT. Must reverse entire grid.

HIGH

Day numbers Western while header uses ٢٠٢٦

Inconsistent numeral system in same component.

HIGH

Navigation arrows <>not mirrored

Previous/next arrows need position swap for RTL.

SCREEN 09 Month Picker Dropdown
HIGH

Month grid flows LTR

يناير starts top-LEFT. Should start top-RIGHT.

HIGH

Year arrows not mirrored

<2026>follows LTR convention.

MEDIUM

Year "2026" Western vs header "٢٠٢٦" Eastern

Mixed numeral systems.

SCREEN 10 Timeline / Schedule View
MEDIUM

Days flow RTL — Partially Correct ✓

Day columns ordered correctly RTL. Time slots within days still flow LTR.

HIGH

Time labels ص/م correct, hours flow LTR

Arabic AM/PM good, but hourly axis direction needs review.

💬

Dialogs & Panels

Standards 02, 03

Dialog titles right-align. Primary actions go LEFT (end of reading flow). All filter labels, search placeholders, and status text must be translated. Checkboxes in filter panels mirror to RIGHT.

SCREEN 11 Audio Recording Modal
HIGH

Modal title "صوتي" left-aligned

Should right-align in RTL.

HIGH

Checkboxes behind modal on LEFT

Confirms wrong-side checkbox placement.

SCREEN 12 Advanced Field Search
CRITICAL

Column header "Email" English

Data table header not translated.

HIGH

Filter label "Email" English

Filter panel shows untranslated field name.

SCREEN 13 Search / Filter Panel
CRITICAL

Address sub-fields ALL English in search

Confirms address labels NEVER translate system-wide.

HIGH

"Regular Search" link English

Should be "بحث عادي".

HIGH

All checkboxes on LEFT of labels

Every filter checkbox sits LEFT of Arabic label.

SCREEN 14 Export Dialog
HIGH

Checkboxes on LEFT of column names

Column selection checkboxes wrong side.

HIGH

Filter icon (▽) on LEFT of search

Leading icon should be RIGHT in RTL.

SCREEN 15 Export Settings — PDF Preview
CRITICAL

"Export Settings >" header English

Settings page header completely English.

HIGH

Radio buttons on LEFT of labels

All radio controls on wrong side.

CRITICAL

PDF preview shows English data

"rose subform" and "September-23" in export preview.

SCREEN 16 Revoke Authorization Dialog
HIGH

English names in Arabic BiDi text

"Zoho CRM Account" / "Crm1" embedded in Arabic creates reading confusion.

🔗

Integrations & File Pickers

Standard 09

Third-party components must inherit RTL direction. File browsers, cloud pickers, and integration panels must translate all UI labels. Embedded widgets cannot be 100% English in an Arabic app.

SCREEN 17 Cloud Picker — 100% English
CRITICAL

Entire component untranslated

"Cloud Picker", "Search", "+ New", "Name", "Attach →", "No files selected" — zero Arabic.

CRITICAL

"Attach →" arrow wrong direction

Right-pointing arrow = proceed in LTR. RTL needs ← direction.

HIGH

Sidebar on LEFT

Cloud service sidebar should be RIGHT in RTL.

SCREEN 18 Multi-File Picker
CRITICAL

File browser entirely English LTR

Same as Cloud Picker: embedded widget ignores RTL completely.

HIGH

Tab labels mixed language

Arabic + English tabs without RTL flow order.

HIGH

"Done" button English

Should be "تم".

SCREEN 19 Integration Management
HIGH

Integration names all English

Product names acceptable, but account descriptions should translate.

LOW

Card grid may flow LTR

First card top-LEFT; should start top-RIGHT in RTL.

SCREEN 20 Connection Details
CRITICAL

Module names LTR-truncated

"...ho CRM Purchase Orders" — truncated from LEFT proves LTR rendering in RTL.

HIGH

"Module : Contacts" English labels

Should be "الوحدة : جهات الاتصال".

HIGH

Navigation arrow (>) wrong direction

Back-navigation chevron points wrong way.

🗺️

Map Report

Standards 02, 09

Map search fields and control labels must translate. Zoom controls and layer selectors should move to the RIGHT side. Map tile content follows provider locale.

SCREEN 21 Map Report
HIGH

"Search Location" English placeholder

Should be "بحث عن موقع".

HIGH

Map controls on LEFT side

Zoom/layer controls should move to RIGHT in RTL.

HIGH

Layer labels: "Basic", "Satellite", "Street" English

Should be Arabic.
